reason: EXERCISE HINT IS CORRECT (lines 433-435): The exercise asks to extend the conservation law to particles undergoing localised collisions, with worldline segments that begin and end at discrete spacetime events. The hint is mathematically correct: when integrating by parts on each finite worldline segment [tau_a, tau_b], the boundary terms p^alpha(tau) phi(gamma(tau)) evaluated at tau_a and tau_b are non-zero. But at each collision vertex, the sum of boundary terms from all incoming and outgoing segments equals sum_n p_n^alpha times phi(x_collision) = phi(x_collision) times sum_n p_n^alpha, which vanishes by four-momentum conservation. For infinite worldline segments (extending to tau -> +/-infinity), the boundary terms vanish by the properness argument. This is correct as stated.
